metadata.title: States PCS: Karnataka GK for KPSC and other Karnataka State Exams

questions.question: In which year Rani Chenamma led revolt against the British East India Company?
questions.explanation: Rani Chenamma led revolt against the British East India Company in the year 1824.

questions.question: What is the state bird of Karnataka ?
questions.explanation: Indian Roller is the state bird of Karnataka. It occurs widely from West Asia to the Indian Subcontinent. It is listed as least concern on the IUCN Red List.

questions.question: Which Karnataka district is known for Silver deposits?
questions.explanation: Chitradurga district in Karnataka is known for deposits of silver and also has deposits of gold and other minerals. These mineral resources contribute to the geological significance of the district. Karnataka’s Raichur district is another area where silver deposits are found. The presence of these deposits is due to the region’s ancient rock formations and mineral veins.

questions.question: Mangalore Port is located in which district of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: New Mangalore Port is an all-weather port situated in Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka. It is the only major port of Karnataka and handles a significant portion of the state’s cargo traffic. The port is located near the city of Mangalore, which is the district headquarters of Dakshina Kannada. Udupi, Hassan, and Uttara Kannada are other districts of Karnataka, but they do not contain Mangalore Port.

questions.question: Which Karnataka airport first got Disabled Aircraft Recovery Equipment (DARE)?
questions.explanation: Kempegowda International Airport in Bangalore became the first in India to install Disabled Aircraft Recovery Equipment (DARE). This equipment enhances operational safety by enabling quick recovery of disabled aircraft on the runway. The airport opened in 2008 and serves as the main international gateway to Karnataka.

questions.question: The Karnataka Vidyavardhaka Sangha was established by:
questions.explanation: The Karnataka Vidyavardhaka Sangha was established by R H Deshpande with the objective of working for the resurgence of the Kannada language which had been marginalised under the rule of the Bombay Presidency where Marathi was the official language.

questions.question: Which among the following writs is issued when the procedure is pending in the court?
questions.explanation: The writ of Prohibition is issued when the procedure is pending in the courts. When the lower court hears any case out of its jurisdiction, then upper court takes over the case by issuing Prohibition writ.

questions.question: Which is not among the powers of Panchayati Raj Institutions in India?
questions.explanation: Judicial Review is not the power of Panchayati Raj Institution. It is a power of Judiciary. The legislative and executive actions are subject to review by the Supreme Court of India in accordance with the Principle of Judicial Review.

questions.question: Adichunchanagir a noted centre of Bhairava worship is in which of the following districts?
questions.explanation: Adichunchanagiri in Mandya district is a noted centre of Bhairava worship on a hill. It was formerly a Natha Pantha centre and is now a seat of the Swamy of the Vokkaliga community.

questions.question: Which of the following places is famous for its native breed of mango called ishaad?
questions.explanation: Ankola is a small town in Uttara Kannada district surrounded by temples, schools, paddy fields and mango groves. It is located on the coast of the Arabian sea and has natural beaches. Ankola is famous for its native breed of Mango called kari Ishaad as wells as its bountiful cashews.
